claimText: Rudrastra is a Hybrid Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) UAV equipped with precision-guided anti-personnel warheads.
evidenceExcerpt: Rudrastra is a Hybrid VTOL UAV equipped with precision-guided anti-personnel warheads.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the specific classification and payload of Rudrastra.
distractorHooks: Describing Rudrastra as an anti-drone system instead of a UAV
Describing Rudrastra as a traditional fixed-wing aircraft without VTOL capabilities
Claiming it is an unarmed surveillance drone

claimText: Bhargavastra is an indigenous 'hard kill' anti-drone system that utilizes a two-layer security system comprising unguided micro-rockets and guided micro-missiles to neutralize drone swarms.
evidenceExcerpt: Bhargavastra is an indigenous 'hard kill' anti-drone system with a two-layer security system (unguided micro-rockets and guided micro-missiles).
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing between 'hard kill' and 'soft kill' anti-drone systems and identifying Bhargavastra's mechanism.
distractorHooks: Classifying Bhargavastra as a 'soft kill' system that uses electronic jamming
Describing Bhargavastra as an offensive drone swarm rather than an anti-drone system

claimText: Fiber optic drones are immune to electronic warfare (EW) jamming and interception because they rely on physical glass tube cables rather than radio links for operation.
evidenceExcerpt: Fiber optic drones use glass tube cables instead of radio links, making them immune to electronic warfare (EW) jamming.
examUse: Conceptual statement-based question on the tactical advantages of fiber optic drones in electronic warfare environments.
distractorHooks: Stating that fiber optic drones use highly encrypted radio frequencies to avoid jamming
Claiming that fiber optic drones are highly susceptible to radio frequency interception

claimText: The use of fiber optic cables in drones allows for faster communication speeds, longer operational distances, and simultaneous communications on different wavelengths.
evidenceExcerpt: Fiber optic cables allow faster speeds, longer distances, and simultaneous communications on different wavelengths.
examUse: Statement-based question evaluating the technical benefits of fiber optic communication over traditional radio links in UAVs.
distractorHooks: Asserting that fiber optic cables limit the operational distance of drones compared to radio links
Claiming fiber optic cables only support single-wavelength communication
